title: Media Types, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds byte-to-Unicode admission ledger from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of byte-to-Unicode admission ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Validate declared content codings and distinguish unsupported, corrupt, truncated, and over-budget representations.
Use a documented media-type and conservative sniff policy while rejecting binary, multipart, and ambiguous content.
Reconcile transport, BOM, HTML declarations, decoder support, replacement characters, and confidence with source offsets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Content Coding Chain
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 3240 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile coding, media, charset, source identity, truncation, and stage-specific digests; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate declared content codings and distinguish unsupported, corrupt, truncated, and over-budget representations.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Content Coding Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate declared content codings and distinguish unsupported, corrupt, truncated, and over-budget representations. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Content Coding Chain, keep Content Coding Chain connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
